title: PaymentAction::Request wire amount is a free-form string: format fossilizes
description: PaymentAction::Request{chain, amount: String} sends the raw CLI input string over the wire. Both payer and payee parse it with parse_zec_to_zatoshi. The format is now part of the wire protocol and any caller (including future Android client) depends on it. If later changed to amount_zatoshi: u64, it's a breaking wire format change. The string form was chosen (implicitly) for human readability; an alternative is to send amount_zatoshi: u64 on the wire and display it locally. Consider whether to lock in the string form before it fossilizes — other chains (Monero, MobileCoin) have different precisions, making a single integer field ambiguous anyway, which is a valid reason to keep the string.
close_reason: Decision: keep string form. Human-readable, chain-agnostic (Monero/MobileCoin precision differs). Format is protocol-stable as-is.
